enrichment.short_description: Open-source, AI-powered workspace for docs, wikis, and projects that you can self-host or run in the cloud.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AppFlowy is an open-source AI workspace for docs, wikis, and projects, with self-hosting and a free cloud plan.

features.title: AppFlowy AI
features.description: A built-in AI assistant that can answer questions, improve writing, and autofill tables and databases.

features.title: Open-source and self-hostable
features.description: The full codebase is open source, so teams can self-host AppFlowy instead of using the hosted cloud service.

features.title: Local AI models
features.description: Supports running local AI models such as Mistral 7B and Llama 3 for privacy-focused, offline AI use.

features.title: Customizable views and databases
features.description: Flexible blocks, views, and properties let users build wikis, grids, and project trackers in one workspace.

features.title: Multi-platform apps
features.description: Native apps are available for macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android, plus a web app.

features.title: Third-party AI model access
features.description: Paid AI add-ons provide access to models including GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude 3.7 Sonnet.

pros: Open-source with a self-hosting option for full data control
Generous free plan that includes a limited number of AI responses
Available on all major desktop and mobile platforms
Offers a local/offline AI option (Vault Workspace) for privacy-sensitive use
cons: Unlimited AI responses and larger workspaces require paid add-ons on top of the base Pro plan
Free plan is capped at a 2-member workspace and limited storage
Advanced AI models (GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Claude 3.7) require a separate paid AI subscription

faqs.question: Is AppFlowy free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, AppFlowy has a free forever plan with a collaborative workspace for up to 2 members, unlimited pages, 5 GB storage, and a limited number of AI responses per month.

faqs.question: Can I self-host AppFlowy?
faqs.answer: Yes, AppFlowy is open source and can be self-hosted instead of using the hosted AppFlowy Cloud.

faqs.question: What AI models does AppFlowy support?
faqs.answer: AppFlowy offers its own built-in AI assistant, local models like Mistral 7B and Llama 3 for offline use, and paid access to third-party models such as GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude 3.7 Sonnet.

faqs.question: What platforms is AppFlowy available on?
faqs.answer: AppFlowy is available on the web and as native apps for macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused team workspace
use_cases.description: Teams that want a shared wiki and project workspace without giving up control of their data can self-host AppFlowy.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted documentation
use_cases.description: Individuals and teams use AppFlowy AI to draft, summarize, and autofill content within docs and databases.

features: 10 AI responses and 2 AI images per month
Access to GPT-5, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and Claude 3.7 Sonnet
Unlimited file size for AI chat

long_form_blocks.heading: What is AppFlowy?
paragraphs: AppFlowy is an open-source workspace application that combines documents, wikis, and project databases with a built-in AI assistant. It is positioned as a customizable, data-ownership-focused alternative to closed workspace tools.

Because the project is open source, teams can either use AppFlowy's hosted cloud service or self-host the platform to keep full control of their data. AppFlowy also supports local AI models for users who want AI features without sending data to third-party providers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who AppFlowy is for
paragraphs: AppFlowy suits teams and individuals who want a flexible workspace for wikis, notes, and lightweight project tracking, especially those who value self-hosting or data privacy over a purely managed SaaS product.

enrichment.short_description: Task manager and to-do list app from the Wunderlist team, combining lists, notes, and AI-assisted planning.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Superlist is a task and note management app from the Wunderlist team, with real-time collaboration and AI features.

features.title: Unified tasks and notes
features.description: Attach contextual notes and details directly to tasks instead of switching between separate apps.

features.title: Nested lists and subtasks
features.description: Break large projects down into nested lists and subtasks for more granular planning.

features.title: Voice-to-task input
features.description: Quickly add tasks by typing or speaking, including converting voice recordings into actionable items.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Share lists with family, friends, or coworkers and collaborate on tasks in real time.

features.title: AI meeting notes and chat
features.description: Higher-tier plans include AI meeting note summarization and AI chat for managing tasks.

features.title: Recurring tasks and widgets
features.description: Set up recurring tasks for routines and use home/lock screen widgets for fast task entry.

pros: Free plan supports unlimited tasks and notes with up to 5 shared lists
Built by the team behind Wunderlist, with a similar list-based workflow
Available across macOS, iOS, Android, and web with offline support
AI features for voice-to-task conversion and meeting note summaries on paid plans
cons: Free plan is capped at 5 private/shared lists and 5 people per shared list
Voice AI and integrations require at least the Basic paid plan
Unlimited AI chat and meeting notes are reserved for the top Super plan

faqs.question: Is Superlist free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Superlist has a free plan that includes unlimited tasks and notes, up to 5 private and shared lists, and up to 5 people per shared list.

faqs.question: Who made Superlist?
faqs.answer: Superlist was created by the team behind Wunderlist, the task management app that was later discontinued.

faqs.question: Does Superlist have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid plans include AI capabilities such as converting voice recordings into tasks, summarizing meeting notes, and an AI chat for task management.

faqs.question: What platforms does Superlist support?
faqs.answer: Superlist is available on macOS, iOS, Android, and the web, with real-time sync and offline support.

use_cases.title: Personal and family task coordination
use_cases.description: Individuals and families use shared lists to coordinate household tasks and errands.

use_cases.title: Team project tracking
use_cases.description: Teams use nested lists, notes, and AI meeting summaries to manage shared work.

features: Up to 5 private and shared lists
Up to 5 people in shared lists
Unlimited lists, sublists, and shared lists

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Superlist?
paragraphs: Superlist is a task management app that combines to-do lists, notes, and project planning in one workspace. It was created by the team that previously built Wunderlist.
The app supports nested lists, subtasks, recurring tasks, and real-time collaboration, along with AI features such as voice-to-task conversion and meeting note summarization on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Superlist is for
paragraphs: Superlist is aimed at individuals, families, and small teams who want a single app for personal to-dos and shared project or household coordination.

enrichment.short_description: A day-planning app with drag-and-drop time blocking, brain dump, and calendar sync for iOS and web.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ellie is a day planner app with time blocking, brain dump, and calendar sync for iOS and web, with a free plan.

features.title: Time blocking
features.description: Drag-and-drop scheduling lets users place tasks directly onto a visual daily calendar.

features.title: Brain dump
features.description: A dedicated space to offload tasks and ideas quickly to free up mental space.

features.title: Calendar integrations
features.description: Syncs with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, and Outlook.

features.title: Recurring tasks
features.description: Supports repeating tasks for routines and rituals.

features.title: Analytics and time tracking
features.description: Visualizes how time is spent across tasks and days.

features.title: Native iOS app
features.description: Includes Apple Calendar and Siri integration, plus iOS widgets and focus mode.

pros: Free plan includes unlimited task creation with no credit card required
Native iOS app with Siri and widget support
Supports Google, Apple, and Outlook calendar sync on paid plan
Built and maintained by a solo, self-funded founder with a stated no-ads, no-data-selling policy
cons: Calendar sync, time boxing, and recurring tasks are locked behind the paid Pro plan
One-time purchase option is priced significantly higher than a year of the monthly subscription
No native Android or dedicated desktop app confirmed

faqs.question: Is Ellie free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ellie has a free plan with unlimited task creation, iOS and web app access, and brain dump, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Ellie Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Ellie Pro is $9.99/month as a subscription, or $299.99 as a one-time purchase for the same features.

faqs.question: Does Ellie sync with my calendar?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ellie Pro integrates with Google Calendar and Apple Calendar.

faqs.question: Who makes Ellie?
faqs.answer: Ellie is built by an independent founder, Chris Raroque, and is self-funded through the founder's other apps.

use_cases.title: Daily time blocking
use_cases.description: Users plan their day by dragging tasks onto a calendar-style timeline.

use_cases.title: iOS-first task management
use_cases.description: iPhone users rely on native Siri and widget integration to plan tasks on the go.

features: Same features as the Pro subscription, paid once

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ellie Planner?
paragraphs: Ellie is a day planner app built around drag-and-drop time blocking and a brain dump feature for offloading tasks. It runs as a native iOS app and a web app.
The free plan covers unlimited task creation, while a paid Pro plan adds calendar sync with Google and Apple Calendar, timebox mode, recurring tasks, and subtasks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ellie is for
paragraphs: Ellie is aimed at individuals who want a simple, visual day-planning system, particularly iPhone users who want native Siri and widget support.

enrichment.short_description: A goal-planning app that organizes tasks across life horizons, from daily boards to multi-year goals.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Timestripe is a goal and task planning app organizing work across daily boards and long-term life horizons.

features.title: Horizons planning system
features.description: Organizes goals across flexible timeframes, from daily tasks to multi-year plans.

features.title: Boards
features.description: Customizable lists for organizing tasks, ideas, and content.

features.title: Climbs
features.description: Step-by-step guides for goal categories such as fitness, finance, and career.

features.title: Timestripe Clock
features.description: A visualization mode intended to help users reflect on how they use their time.

features.title: Rich notes
features.description: Notes can embed video, images, files, and maps alongside tasks and goals.

features.title: Shared templates
features.description: Includes shared templates and checklists for common planning use cases.

pros: Free plan available with sync across devices
Distinct long-term "horizons" planning model beyond typical daily to-do lists
Available on web, iPhone/iPad, and Android
One-time multi-year plan option for users who prefer not to subscribe monthly
cons: Free plan limits goals per horizon and boards, and caps file uploads at 10MB per file
No confirmed native Mac or Windows desktop app, only a web app
Reviewers note limited integrations with other productivity tools

faqs.question: Is Timestripe free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Timestripe has a free plan with limited goals per horizon, limited boards, and sync across devices.

faqs.question: How much does Timestripe Pro cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan is $5 per user per month and removes the limits on goals, boards, and climbs from the free plan.

faqs.question: Where is Timestripe based?
faqs.answer: Timestripe is operated by Timestripe OÜ, headquartered in Tallinn, Estonia.

faqs.question: What platforms does Timestripe support?
faqs.answer: Timestripe is available as a web app and on iPhone, iPad, and Android.

use_cases.title: Long-term goal planning
use_cases.description: Users break down multi-year goals into yearly, monthly, and daily boards.

use_cases.title: Guided goal progress
use_cases.description: Users follow step-by-step Climbs to work toward goals like fitness or career milestones.

features: Same features as the PRO plan

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Timestripe?
paragraphs: Timestripe is a goal-planning application that organizes tasks and goals across flexible time horizons, from daily boards up to multi-year plans. It combines a calendar view, customizable boards, and rich notes.
The app is built by Timestripe OÜ, based in Tallinn, Estonia, and is available on the web, iPhone, iPad, and Android.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Timestripe is for
paragraphs: Timestripe suits individuals who want to plan beyond simple daily to-do lists, connecting everyday tasks to longer-term goals and life horizons.

enrichment.short_description: A minimalist weekly planner combining a to-do list with a calendar, with free and premium tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tweek is a minimalist weekly planner combining a to-do list and calendar, with Google/Apple Calendar sync.

features.title: Weekly board layout
features.description: Displays tasks in a paper-planner-style weekly view alongside a 'Someday' column for unscheduled items.

features.title: Calendar sync
features.description: Premium syncs with Google Calendar and Apple Calendar, plus Apple Reminders.

features.title: Shared calendars
features.description: Users can share schedules with clients, partners, teammates, or family members.

features.title: Recurring tasks and subtasks
features.description: Premium adds recurring tasks, subtasks, and file attachments.

features.title: Printable planner
features.description: Weekly plans can be printed for a paper-planner-style workflow.

features.title: API access
features.description: Offers read/write API access, with expanded limits on the Premium plan.

pros: Permanent free plan with 2 active calendars and 3 Someday columns
Simple, minimalist weekly-view interface
Calendar sync with both Google and Apple Calendar on Premium
Native mobile apps on the App Store and Google Play
cons: Free plan limits calendars, Someday columns, and custom colors
Calendar sync and reminders integration require the paid Premium plan
No monthly-view or theme customization on the free tier

faqs.question: Is Tweek free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tweek has a permanent free plan with 2 active calendars, 3 Someday columns, and 2 custom colors.

faqs.question: How much is Tweek Premium?
faqs.answer: Tweek Premium costs $5.99/month or $49.99/year.

faqs.question: Does Tweek sync with Google or Apple Calendar?
faqs.answer: Yes, calendar sync with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, and Apple Reminders is available on the Premium plan.

faqs.question: Is Tweek available on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tweek has apps on the App Store and Google Play, in addition to the web app.

use_cases.title: Weekly personal planning
use_cases.description: Individuals use the weekly board and Someday column to plan tasks in a paper-planner style.

use_cases.title: Shared family or team scheduling
use_cases.description: Families and small teams share calendars to coordinate schedules and responsibilities.

features: Unlimited calendars, Someday columns, and colors

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Tweek?
paragraphs: Tweek is a minimalist weekly planner that combines a to-do list with a calendar view. Its layout resembles a paper planner, with a weekly board and a 'Someday' column for unscheduled tasks.
A free plan is available indefinitely, while the Premium plan adds calendar sync with Google Calendar and Apple Calendar, recurring tasks, and file attachments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Tweek is for
paragraphs: Tweek is aimed at individuals, families, and small teams who want a simple, minimalist alternative to a paper planner with optional calendar sync.

enrichment.short_description: An encrypted, all-in-one productivity app combining tasks, habits, notes, and mood tracking, built for focus and privacy.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lunatask combines encrypted task management, habit tracking, and journaling in one app, with a free plan available.

features.title: Automatic task prioritization
features.description: Prioritizes tasks using methods like Kanban, Must/Should/Want, and the Eisenhower Matrix.

features.title: Calendar integration
features.description: Time blocking with Google, Outlook, and iCloud calendar support, plus joining Zoom, Meet, or Teams calls directly.

features.title: Habit tracking
features.description: Tracks daily and weekly habits with streak counters and reminder notifications.

features.title: Mood and wellness tracking
features.description: Logs emotion, energy level, and stress to help users spot patterns over time.

features.title: Encrypted notes and journaling
features.description: Markdown-based notebooks with templates and optional PIN protection.

features.title: Relationship tracker
features.description: A lightweight personal CRM with reconnect reminders for maintaining relationships.

pros: End-to-end encryption for tasks, notes, and journals
Combines tasks, habits, mood tracking, and notes in one app rather than several
Available on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android
Offers a one-time lifetime license as an alternative to subscribing
cons: Free plan limits users to 2 areas of life, 7 daily habits, and 30 relationships
Calendar integrations and advanced trackers require the paid Premium plan
Lifetime license is not available on mobile or the Mac App Store

faqs.question: Is Lunatask free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lunatask has a free plan limited to 2 areas of life, 7 daily habits, and 30 relationships, with basic features.

faqs.question: How much does Lunatask Premium cost?
faqs.answer: Premium is $6/month billed annually, or $8/month billed monthly, and unlocks unlimited features and calendar integrations.

faqs.question: Does Lunatask offer a lifetime license?
faqs.answer: Yes, a one-time lifetime license is available for $300, though it is not available on mobile or the Mac App Store.

faqs.question: Is Lunatask data encrypted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Lunatask emphasizes end-to-end encryption for tasks, notes, and journals.

use_cases.title: ADHD-friendly task management
use_cases.description: Users prioritize tasks automatically and track habits to reduce decision fatigue.

use_cases.title: Private life tracking
use_cases.description: Individuals track mood, habits, and relationships in one encrypted app.

features: Basic task, habit, and note features
Not available on mobile or Mac App Store

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lunatask?
paragraphs: Lunatask is an all-in-one productivity app that combines task management, habit tracking, journaling, and mood tracking with end-to-end encryption. It is designed with ADHD-friendly workflows in mind.
The app is available on Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android, with a free plan and a Premium subscription that unlocks calendar integrations and unlimited tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lunatask is for
paragraphs: Lunatask is aimed at individuals with ADHD, students, teachers, and privacy-conscious users who want tasks, habits, and personal tracking combined in one encrypted app.

enrichment.short_description: A highly customizable task manager built for ADHD and procrastination, with 100+ configurable productivity features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Amazing Marvin is a customizable task manager for ADHD, with focus modes, time tracking, and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Super Focus Mode
features.description: Shows only one task at a time to reduce overwhelm and decision fatigue.

features.title: Task Jar
features.description: Randomly selects a task from a list to help overcome decision paralysis.

features.title: Procrastination Wizard
features.description: Walks users through breaking a task into smaller, more approachable steps.

features.title: Day planning with capacity feedback
features.description: Schedules tasks onto a day view while giving feedback on planned workload.

features.title: Pomodoro timer and time tracking
features.description: Includes a customizable Pomodoro timer and time tracking per task.

features.title: Accountability Pledge
features.description: Lets users put financial stakes on hitting task deadlines.

pros: Extremely customizable, with 100+ features and multiple view layouts (Kanban, list, grid, calendar)
Built specifically around ADHD and procrastination-focused workflows
Available on Mac, Windows, Web, iOS, and Android
Offers pay-what-you-can pricing and a student discount for those facing financial barriers
cons: No free tier; only a 14-day free trial before requiring a subscription
Extensive customization options can create a learning curve for new users
Specific pricing tiers beyond the single Pro plan are not published on the site

faqs.question: Does Amazing Marvin have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Amazing Marvin does not have a free tier, but it offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Amazing Marvin cost?
faqs.answer: Marvin Pro costs $8/month, or $96/year when billed annually (a 33% saving).

faqs.question: Who is Amazing Marvin designed for?
faqs.answer: It is designed for people with ADHD, chronic procrastinators, and anyone wanting a highly personalized productivity system.

faqs.question: Is there a student discount?
faqs.answer: Yes, Amazing Marvin offers a 50% student discount available upon request, plus a pay-what-you-can option for those facing financial hardship.

use_cases.title: Overcoming task overwhelm
use_cases.description: Users rely on Super Focus Mode and the Task Jar to reduce decision fatigue around what to work on next.
use_cases.audience: People with ADHD or chronic procrastination

use_cases.title: Customized productivity workflows
use_cases.description: Users build a personalized system by combining Marvin's many optional features and views.
use_cases.audience: Productivity enthusiasts who want a tailored system

features: Multi-platform access (web, Mac, Windows, iOS, Android)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Amazing Marvin?
paragraphs: Amazing Marvin is a task management app built around deep customization, offering more than 100 optional features such as Super Focus Mode, a Task Jar, and a Procrastination Wizard. It was created by a founder addressing her own struggles with overwhelm in existing task managers.
The app is subscription-based with a single Pro plan, offered after a 14-day free trial, and is available on Mac, Windows, Web, iOS, and Android.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Amazing Marvin is for
paragraphs: Amazing Marvin is aimed at people with ADHD, chronic procrastinators, and anyone who wants to build a highly personalized task management workflow rather than use a one-size-fits-all app.

enrichment.short_description: A hyper-scheduling app for Mac and iOS that unifies tasks, calendar events, and notes into one timeline.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sorted 3 combines tasks, calendar events, and notes into a unified timeline for Mac and iOS hyper-scheduling.

features.title: Unified timeline
features.description: Combines tasks, calendar events, and notes into a single scheduling timeline.

features.title: Auto-scheduling
features.description: Automatically places tasks into available time slots on the timeline.

features.title: Time Ruler
features.description: A visual time-blocking tool for planning the day in detail.

features.title: Support for simple and complex workflows
features.description: Works for basic task checking as well as more complex project management.

pros: 4.8-star App Store rating across 21,000+ reviews
Free tier available on both iOS and macOS before upgrading to PRO
Recognized as App Store App of the Day in 130+ regions
Time-blocking and auto-scheduling combine tasks, events, and notes in one view
cons: PRO unlock is priced separately on iOS ($14.99) and macOS ($24.99)
Only available on Apple platforms (iOS and macOS), no Android, Windows, or web version
Limited company information published on the official site

faqs.question: Is Sorted 3 free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sorted 3 has a free tier on both iOS and macOS, with a paid PRO unlock for additional features.

faqs.question: How much is Sorted 3 PRO?
faqs.answer: PRO is $14.99 on iOS and $24.99 on macOS.

faqs.question: What platforms is Sorted 3 available on?
faqs.answer: Sorted 3 is available on iOS and macOS only.

faqs.question: What does Sorted 3 do?
faqs.answer: It combines tasks, calendar events, and notes into a unified timeline, with auto-scheduling and time-blocking tools.

use_cases.title: Hyper-scheduled daily planning
use_cases.description: Users plan their entire day, including tasks and events, on one unified timeline.
use_cases.audience: Students and professionals seeking detailed scheduling

use_cases.title: ADHD-friendly time blocking
use_cases.description: Users with ADHD use the Time Ruler and auto-scheduling to structure their day visually.

features: Core task, calendar, and note features
Full unified timeline and auto-scheduling features
Core task, calendar, and note features
Full unified timeline and auto-scheduling features

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sorted 3?
paragraphs: Sorted 3 is a Mac and iOS app that combines tasks, calendar events, and notes into a single scheduling timeline, enabling what it calls 'hyper-scheduling' of daily activities.
It offers a free tier on both platforms, with a one-time PRO unlock that adds the full auto-scheduling and time-blocking feature set.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sorted 3 is for
paragraphs: Sorted 3 targets students, professionals, and people with ADHD who want to schedule tasks, events, and notes together in one detailed timeline rather than juggling separate apps.

enrichment.short_description: A markdown-based notes, tasks, and calendar app storing plaintext files, with AI note tools for Mac, iOS, and web.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: NotePlan combines markdown notes, tasks, and a calendar in one plaintext-based app, with a 7-day free trial.

features.title: Daily, weekly, and monthly notes
features.description: Digital planning notes integrated with calendar views for different timeframes.

features.title: Time-blocking
features.description: Schedule tasks directly onto the calendar with drag-and-drop time blocking.

features.title: Bi-directional linking
features.description: Link notes together and reference tasks or notes across the workspace.

features.title: Plaintext markdown storage
features.description: All content is stored as plaintext markdown files, enabling portability and offline access.

features.title: Google Calendar sync
features.description: View and create Google Calendar events directly inside NotePlan.

features.title: AI note tools
features.description: Includes a Smart Note Assistant for writing/summarizing and Memo AI, which turns voice notes into structured insights.

features.title: Plugins and templates
features.description: Supports JavaScript-based plugins, custom templates, and themes for automation and formatting.

pros: Data is stored as plaintext markdown, avoiding lock-in to a proprietary format
Combines notes, tasks, and calendar in a single app rather than separate tools
AI features for voice transcription and note summarization
Ranked among Apple's Top 10 Notes apps
cons: No free tier; only a 7-day free trial before requiring a subscription
Primarily built for the Apple ecosystem (Mac, iPhone, iPad), with a web app as the main non-Apple option
No confirmed native Windows, Android, or Linux app

faqs.question: Does NotePlan have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, NotePlan does not have a permanent free plan, but it offers a 7-day free trial.

faqs.question: How much does NotePlan cost?
faqs.answer: NotePlan is $8.33/month billed annually ($99.99/year), or $12/month billed monthly.

faqs.question: What platforms does NotePlan support?
faqs.answer: NotePlan is available on Mac, iPhone, iPad, and as a web app.

faqs.question: How is my data stored in NotePlan?
faqs.answer: NotePlan stores notes as plaintext markdown files, which sync across devices and support offline access.

use_cases.title: Unified planning and note-taking
use_cases.description: Professionals combine daily notes, tasks, and calendar events in one workspace to avoid switching apps.

use_cases.title: Voice-to-note capture
use_cases.description: Users record voice notes on iPhone that are transcribed and structured with AI.

features: All core notes, tasks, and calendar features
All core notes, tasks, and calendar features

long_form_blocks.heading: What is NotePlan?
paragraphs: NotePlan is a productivity app that combines daily notes, task management, and calendar views, storing all content as plaintext markdown files. It has been available since 2016.
The app includes AI-assisted features such as a Smart Note Assistant for writing and summarizing, and Memo AI for turning voice recordings into structured notes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who NotePlan is for
paragraphs: NotePlan is built for tech-savvy professionals and entrepreneurs on Mac and iOS who want notes, tasks, and calendar events unified in a single, markdown-based workspace.

enrichment.short_description: A markdown notes app for Mac and iOS with tagging, iCloud sync, and export to multiple formats.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bear is a markdown notes app for Mac and iOS with tags, iCloud sync, and export to PDF, DOCX, and more.

features.title: Markdown editing
features.description: Write notes with markdown formatting, including text, photos, tables, and to-do lists.

features.title: Tag-based organization
features.description: Organize notes with nested tags and choose from over 250 icon options.

features.title: Multi-format export
features.description: Export notes as TXT, Markdown, PDF, HTML, DOCX, ePub, or JPG.

features.title: Document scanning and OCR
features.description: Scan documents into notes and search scanned or handwritten text via OCR.

features.title: iCloud sync
features.description: Syncs notes across Apple devices via iCloud, available on the Pro plan.

features.title: Themes and customization
features.description: Includes 28+ themes and 15 app icon options for personalizing the interface.

features.title: Note encryption
features.description: Individual notes can be encrypted for added privacy.

pros: Free tier available with local access, document scanning, and basic export
Won an Apple Design Award (2017) and App of the Year (2016)
Deep markdown and export format support, including DOCX and ePub
7-day free trial before the Pro subscription begins
cons: iCloud sync and full export formats require the paid Pro plan
Only available on Mac, iPhone, and iPad, with no Windows or Android app
Free tier is limited to 3 themes and 1 app icon

faqs.question: Is Bear free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bear has a free tier with local note access, document scanning, basic export, 3 themes, and 1 app icon.

faqs.question: How much does Bear Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Bear Pro is $2.99/month or $29.99/year, after a 7-day free trial.

faqs.question: What platforms does Bear support?
faqs.answer: Bear is available on Mac, iPhone, and iPad only.

faqs.question: Who makes Bear?
faqs.answer: Bear is developed by Shiny Frog.

use_cases.title: Everyday markdown note-taking
use_cases.description: Users capture and organize notes with markdown formatting and tags across Apple devices.

use_cases.title: Writing and long-form documents
use_cases.description: Writers use Bear's export formats and organization to draft and export longer documents.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bear?
paragraphs: Bear is a markdown note-taking app for Apple devices, focused on a simple, polished writing experience with tag-based organization and multiple export formats.
It offers a free tier for local note-taking, with a Pro subscription that adds iCloud sync, additional themes, and OCR search over scanned documents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bear is for
paragraphs: Bear suits Apple users who want an elegant, markdown-based notes app, from casual note-takers to writers managing longer documents and projects.
